formulas.name: The Five Ws (Who, What, Where, When, Why)
formulas.formula: Identify the essential information in a report by considering the following questions: Who, What, Where, When, and Why
formulas.variables: The key elements of a report that need to be answered
formulas.notes: Use these questions to structure your report and ensure it includes all necessary information

formulas.name: Introduction-Body-Conclusion (IBC) Structure
formulas.formula: A report should have a clear introduction, body, and conclusion to convey the message effectively
formulas.variables: The three main sections of a report
formulas.notes: Use the IBC structure to organize your report and make it easy to follow

formulas.name: The INFORM Method
formulas.formula: Use the INFORM method to write a clear and concise report by focusing on the following elements: Introduction, Narrative, Facts, Observation, and Recommendation
formulas.variables: The key elements of a report that need to be included
formulas.notes: Use the INFORM method to structure your report and make it engaging

formulas.name: The STAR Method
formulas.formula: Use the STAR method to write a report by focusing on the following elements: Situation, Task, Action, and Result
formulas.variables: The key elements of a report that need to be included
formulas.notes: Use the STAR method to structure your report and make it effective

formulas.name: Clarity and Conciseness
formulas.formula: Use clear and concise language to convey the message effectively and avoid ambiguity
formulas.variables: The key elements of a report that need to be considered
formulas.notes: Use clear and concise language to make your report easy to understand

formulas.name: Tone and Audience
formulas.formula: Use the appropriate tone and language to suit your audience and purpose
formulas.variables: The key elements of a report that need to be considered
formulas.notes: Use the appropriate tone and language to engage your audience and convey the message effectively
